title: senecio acutifolius
intro: Senecio acutifolius, also known as Tree Groundsel, is a shrubby perennial plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its tall stem and narrow, pointed leaves. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, with its leaves containing toxic compounds known as pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Senecio acutifolius include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use requires careful dosing and caution due to its potential toxicity.
